var-cluster.name: cluster
var-cluster.queryValue: SELECT DISTINCT JSONExtractString(labels, 'cluster') as value FROM signoz_metrics.distributed_time_series_v4_1day WHERE metric_name = 'cnpg_collector_up' ORDER BY value ASC

var-namespace.name: namespace
var-namespace.queryValue: SELECT DISTINCT JSONExtractString(labels, 'namespace') as value FROM signoz_metrics.distributed_time_series_v4_1day WHERE metric_name = 'cnpg_collector_up' ORDER BY value ASC

widgets.description: Section: Cluster Overview
widgets.title: Cluster Overview

widgets.description: 1 if PostgreSQL is up and the collector can reach it, 0 otherwise. Grouped by cluster and pod.
widgets.title: Instances Up

widgets.description: Number of streaming replicas connected to this primary instance.
widgets.title: Streaming Replicas

widgets.description: 1 if a manual switchover is required (e.g. after primary failure with no automatic failover), 0 otherwise.
widgets.title: Manual Switchover Required

widgets.description: 1 if the instance is fenced (isolated from the network), 0 otherwise. A fenced instance will not serve connections.
widgets.title: Fencing Active

widgets.description: Section: WAL & Replication
widgets.title: WAL & Replication

widgets.description: Replication lag in seconds between the primary and each replica. High values indicate replicas are falling behind.
widgets.title: Replication Lag (seconds)

widgets.description: Total size in bytes of WAL segment files in the pg_wal directory. Sustained growth may indicate archiving problems.
widgets.title: WAL Directory Size

widgets.description: Total WAL data generated in bytes (PostgreSQL 14+). Useful for estimating write amplification and storage requirements.
widgets.title: WAL Generation Rate

widgets.description: Number of WAL segments ready to be archived. A growing count indicates the WAL archiver is not keeping up.
widgets.title: WAL Archive Queue (Ready / Done)

widgets.description: WAL bytes difference between primary and each replication slot. Large values indicate slot consumers are lagging and may cause WAL accumulation.
widgets.title: Replication Slot Lag (bytes)

widgets.description: Section: Connections
widgets.title: Connections

widgets.description: Total number of backend connections grouped by database, user, application, and state. Helps identify which clients are consuming connections.
widgets.title: Active Connections by State

widgets.description: Number of backends waiting on locks. A sustained value above 0 indicates lock contention.
widgets.title: Connections Waiting on Locks

widgets.description: Maximum duration in seconds of any currently open transaction. Long-running transactions can block autovacuum and cause table bloat.
widgets.title: Longest Open Transaction (seconds)

widgets.description: Section: Performance
widgets.title: Performance

widgets.description: Rate of committed transactions per second across all databases. A drop may indicate application problems or reduced load.
widgets.title: Transaction Commits / sec

widgets.description: Rate of rolled-back transactions per second. A spike may indicate application errors, deadlocks, or connection timeouts.
widgets.title: Transaction Rollbacks / sec

widgets.description: Rate of rows inserted per second across all databases.
widgets.title: Tuples Inserted / sec

widgets.description: Rate of rows updated per second. High update rates combined with low autovacuum activity can cause table bloat.
widgets.title: Tuples Updated / sec

widgets.description: Rate of rows deleted per second.
widgets.title: Tuples Deleted / sec

widgets.description: Rate of rows returned by queries (full scans + index scans). A high ratio of tup_returned vs tup_fetched may indicate missing indexes.
widgets.title: Tuples Fetched vs Returned / sec

widgets.description: Section: Storage
widgets.title: Storage

widgets.description: Disk size of each database in bytes. Monitor for growth trends to plan storage capacity.
widgets.title: Database Size

widgets.description: Number of temporary files created by query sort/hash operations. High values may indicate insufficient work_mem.
widgets.title: Temp Files Created / sec

widgets.description: Amount of data written to temporary files by queries. Large values with high work_mem may indicate complex query plans.
widgets.title: Temp Storage Written / sec
